hints.text: **Black car, no antenna, no red brake light**
hints.uniq_note: The only Middle-Eastern country covered by a black car without antenna or brake light — elsewhere in the world black cars exist, so confirm with Arabic-only signage.

hints.text: Plates **white** (long or short); taxis/commercial = **green strip left**

shared_with.note: UAE also uses long white plates.
shared_with.diff: The **green strip** is Jordan-only in the Middle East; UAE has English on most signs, Jordan doesn't.

shared_with.note: Israel = yellow plates; the West Bank mixes white domestic + yellow Israeli.
shared_with.diff: Yellow plates → Israel; Hebrew script → Israel.

hints.text: **Arabic** — and unlike the UAE, most commercial signs have **no English**

shared_with.note: Arabic too — but with English on most commercial signage.

hints.text: Chevrons **white with red arrows** (Israel: black w/ white arrows)
Poles: rounded concrete w/ **diagonal support** or **3 horizontal Y-tops**; thin metal poles w/ 2 supports + 3 vertical coils
Very **arid**, much lifeless desert — even the lushest parts are only arid-Mediterranean

shared_with.note: Jerusalem & West Bank use similar sandstone.
shared_with.diff: Hebrew or yellow plates → Israel side.

hints.text: Road quality often poor — heat-warped, **buckled cracks** (generally worse than Israel)
Coverage only in the **far west**: bigger towns + main roads; ends N of Jerash (Irbid = zero coverage), south to Aqaba
Largest city w/ extensive coverage: big-city feel, slightly hilly, medium apartment blocks; taxis **yellow-green**

bullets: **15** (longest divided, N-S): desert; rocky hills near Aqaba
**40** (west of Amman, divided): low green shrubs, hills → sandy Dead-Sea side; **Y-shaped lampposts**
**65 north**: Dead Sea directly west + long flat ridge across
**65 south**: single-lane N-S, long sandy stretches south of Feifa (some overcast; sunset near Aqaba airport)
**80**: divided valley road between rigid mountains, runs **E-W** (vs 15's N-S)

hints.text: Flat, sometimes tilled fields w/ **rich red soil** (continues N+S along Hwy 35); taxis yellow w/ **white bumpers**
**Wadi al Hasa** (Hwy 35): arid rolling-hill valley, smooth rounded rock, single-lane road; **Aqaba**: jagged mountain wall east/south + olive-green/black taxis
Wadi Musa: **bulbous rock formations**; Petra trekkers: eroded reddish rock, narrow gorges, tourist crowds (a mainstay of the default World map!)
